title: Kotschya uniflora
taxonomy: Kotschya uniflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Kotschya. The genus Kotschya is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical Africa. Within the family Fabaceae, Kotschya uniflora is also known as a legume. The exact classification of Kotschya uniflora may vary slightly depending on the specific taxonomic system being used.

intro: Kotschya uniflora, commonly known as the African bushwillow, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Kotschya uniflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
